---
title: "Analyses_Surfaces"
output: html_document
date: "2025-08-11"
---


```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo   =FALSE,      
                      include=TRUE ,     
                      message=FALSE,      
                      warning=FALSE,      
                      comment=NA,         
                      fig.align="center", 
                      fig.height = 5,     
                      fig.widht = 5      
                      )
```


```{r}
# Load packages
library(readr)      
library(tidyverse)
library(xtable)
library(readxl)       
library(dplyr)      
library(ggplot2)     
library(tidyr)       
library(purrr)       
library(stringr)     
library(forcats)     
library(broom)       
library(knitr)       
library(kableExtra)  
library(ggpubr)
library(rstatix)
library(car) 
library(scales) 
library(mgcv)
library(brms)
library(patchwork)
```

```{r}
# Load the processed data
reproduction <- readRDS("Data/Processed_Data/reproduction.rds") # This is for reproduction
surfaces <- readRDS("Data/Processed_Data/surfaces.rds") # This is for survival 
merged_data <- readRDS("Data/Processed_Data/merged_data.rds")
```


```{r data-overview, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
#function to create data structure overview
create_data_overview <- function(data, dataset_name) {
  data.frame(
    Variable = names(data),
    Type = sapply(data, class)
  ) %>%
    kable(caption = dataset_name) %>%
    kable_styling(full_width = FALSE, 
                  bootstrap_options = "striped",
                  position = "left")
}

# Show structure of both datasets
create_data_overview(reproduction, "Reproduction Dataset Structure")
create_data_overview(surfaces, "Surfaces Dataset Structure")
```


```{r survival-plot, echo=FALSE, fig.height=5, fig.width=7, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
#Define custom colors
custom_colors <- c("Control" = "#1f77b4",
                   "Copper" = "#ff7f0e",
                   "Plastic" = "#2ca02c",
                   "Steel" = "#9932CC")

phagesurvivalPlot <- ggplot(surfaces, aes(x = Time, y = log10(VirusTiter), color = Surface, shape = Surface)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_line(aes(group = interaction(Surface, Experiment)), alpha = 0.3) +
  facet_grid(PhageType ~ Temperature, scales = "free_y",
             labeller = labeller(Temperature = function(x) paste0(x, "C"))) +
  scale_color_manual(values = custom_colors) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c(16, 17, 15, 18)) +
  labs(#title = "Phage Survival on Different Surfaces and Temperatures",
       x = "Time (hours)",
       y = expression(log[10]~"Virus Titer"),
       color = "Surface",
       shape = "Surface") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 16),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.box = "horizontal",
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_rect(color = "grey80", fill = NA),
    axis.text = element_text(color = "black"),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold")
  ) +
  guides(color = guide_legend(nrow = 1, override.aes = list(size = 4, alpha = 1)),
         shape = guide_legend(nrow = 1, override.aes = list(size = 4, alpha = 1)))

#Saving the plot
png("PhageSurvivalPlot.png", width=6, height=4, units="in", res=300)
print(phagesurvivalPlot)
dev.off() #png form

ggsave("PhageSurvivalPlot.pdf", plot = phagesurvivalPlot, width = 6, height = 4, units = "in") #pdf form
```


```{r}
#  Differences in survival rates for PhiX174 and T4 at different surfaces and temperatures?
# Filter data for the two temperatures of interest: 4°C and 37°C
filtered_surfaces <- surfaces %>%
  filter(Temperature %in% c(4, 37)) %>%
  mutate(
    PhageType = factor(PhageType),
    Surface = factor(Surface),
    Temperature = factor(Temperature, levels = c("4", "37"))
  )

# Define a custom color palette for the surfaces
surface_colors <- c(
  "Control" = "#1f78b4",
  "Copper"  = "#e31a1c",
  "Plastic" = "#33a02c",
  "Steel"   = "#9932CC"
)

# Define shapes for surfaces
surface_shapes <- c(
  "Control" = 16,
  "Copper"  = 17,
  "Plastic" = 15,
  "Steel"   = 18
)

# Plot
T4PhiXComparison <- ggplot(filtered_surfaces, aes(x = Time, y = VirusTiter, color = Surface, shape = Surface)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_line(aes(group = interaction(Surface, PhageType, Temperature, Experiment)), alpha = 0.5) +
  facet_grid(PhageType ~ Temperature, 
             labeller = labeller(Temperature = function(x) paste0(x, "°C"))) +
  scale_y_log10("Virus Titer (log scale)") +
  scale_color_manual(values = surface_colors) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = surface_shapes) +
  labs(
    title = "Survival of PhiX174 vs. T4 at 4°C and 37°C Across Different Surfaces",
    x = "Time (hours)",
    color = "Surface",
    shape = "Surface"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold")
  )

#Saving the plot
png("T4PhiXComparisonPlot.png", width=6, height=4, units="in", res=300)
print(T4PhiXComparison)
dev.off() #png form

ggsave("T4PhiXComparisonPlot.pdf", plot = T4PhiXComparison, width = 6, height = 4, units = "in") #pdf form
```


```{r}
# Survival rate, assumption checks, 
# Non-parametric analysis
# Calculate survival rate relative to initial titer (time 0)
survival_data <- surfaces %>%
  group_by(Experiment, PhageType, Temperature, Surface) %>%
  mutate(
    InitialTiter = VirusTiter[Time == 0],
    SurvivalRate = VirusTiter / InitialTiter * 100
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

# Function to check normality and homogeneity of variance
check_assumptions <- function(data, surface) {
  filtered_data <- data %>%
    filter(Surface == surface, Time == 8)  # Looking at final timepoint
  
  # Shapiro test for each group with error handling
  normality_tests <- filtered_data %>%
    group_by(PhageType, Temperature) %>%
    summarise(
      n = n(),
      mean = mean(SurvivalRate),
      sd = sd(SurvivalRate),
      shapiro_p = tryCatch(
        shapiro.test(SurvivalRate)$p.value,
        error = function(e) NA
      )
    ) %>%
    mutate(
      normality_status = case_when(
        is.na(shapiro_p) & sd == 0 ~ "Constant values",
        is.na(shapiro_p) ~ "Test failed",
        shapiro_p > 0.05 ~ "Normal",
        TRUE ~ "Non-normal"
      )
    )
  
  # Levene's test with error handling
  levene_result <- tryCatch(
    car::leveneTest(SurvivalRate ~ interaction(PhageType, Temperature), 
                    data = filtered_data),
    error = function(e) "Levene's test failed - likely due to constant values"
  )
  
  list(normality = normality_tests, variance = levene_result)
}
```


```{r}
# Create visualization of survival curves
create_survival_plot <- function(data, surface_name) {
  ggplot(data %>% filter(Surface == surface_name), 
         aes(x = Time, y = SurvivalRate + 0.01,  
             color = interaction(PhageType, Temperature, sep = " "),
             shape = interaction(PhageType, Temperature, sep = " "))) +
    stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "point", size = 2) +
    stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "line") +
    stat_summary(fun.data = mean_se, geom = "errorbar", width = 0.2) +
    scale_y_log10(labels = scales::label_number()) +
    labs(
      x = "Time (hours)", 
      y = "Survival Rate (%)", 
      color = "Phage Type & Temperature",
      shape = "Phage Type & Temperature"
    ) +
    theme_pubr() +
    ggtitle(paste("Virus Survival Rate on", surface_name)) +
    theme(legend.position = "right")
}

#Saving the plot
png("SurvivalPlot.png", width=6, height=4, units="in", res=300)
print(create_survival_plot)
dev.off() #png form
```


```{r}
# Function for non-parametric analysis
analyze_surface <- function(data, surface_name) {
  surface_data <- data %>%
    filter(Surface == surface_name, Time == 8)  
  
  # Kruskal-Wallis test for each factor
  kw_phage <- kruskal.test(SurvivalRate ~ PhageType, data = surface_data)
  kw_temp <- kruskal.test(SurvivalRate ~ Temperature, data = surface_data)
  
  # Wilcoxon rank sum tests for pairwise comparisons
  pairwise_phage <- pairwise.wilcox.test(
    surface_data$SurvivalRate,
    interaction(surface_data$PhageType, surface_data$Temperature),
    p.adjust.method = "bonferroni"
  )
  
  list(
    kw_phage = kw_phage,
    kw_temp = kw_temp,
    pairwise = pairwise_phage
  )
}

# Check assumptions for each surface
surfaces_list <- unique(surfaces$Surface)
assumption_results <- lapply(surfaces_list, function(s) check_assumptions(survival_data, s))
names(assumption_results) <- surfaces_list

# Create plots for each surface
survival_plots <- lapply(surfaces_list, function(s) create_survival_plot(survival_data, s))
names(survival_plots) <- surfaces_list

# Perform analysis for each surface
statistical_results <- lapply(surfaces_list, function(s) analyze_surface(survival_data, s))
names(statistical_results) <- surfaces_list

# Print results for inspection
print("Assumption check results:")
print(assumption_results)

print("Statistical analysis results:")
print(statistical_results)
```


```{r}
# # Create survival curve plot
# survival_plot <- ggplot(survival_data, 
#                         aes(x = Time, y = SurvivalRate + 0.01, 
#                             color = PhageType,
#                             linetype = factor(Temperature),
#                             shape = PhageType)) +
#   geom_point(size = 2) +
#   stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "line") +
#   scale_y_log10(labels = scales::label_number()) +
#   facet_wrap(~Surface, scales = "free_y") +
#   labs(
#     x = "Time (hours)",
#     y = "Survival Rate (%)",
#     color = "Phage Type",
#     linetype = "Temperature (°C)",
#     shape = "Phage Type"
#   ) +
#   theme_pubr() +
#   scale_linetype_manual(values = c("solid", "dashed")) +
#   theme(
#     legend.position = "bottom",
#     strip.background = element_rect(fill = "white"),
#     strip.text = element_text(face = "bold")
#   )

# Create summary statistics table
survival_summary <- survival_data %>%
  group_by(Surface, PhageType, Temperature) %>%
  summarise(
    Initial_Titer = mean(VirusTiter[Time == 0]),
    Final_Titer = mean(VirusTiter[Time == 8]),
    Survival_Rate = mean(SurvivalRate[Time == 8]),
    SD = sd(SurvivalRate[Time == 8])
  ) %>%
  arrange(Surface, Temperature, PhageType)

# Create formatted table
survival_table <- survival_summary %>%
  mutate(
    Initial_Titer = formatC(Initial_Titer, format = "e", digits = 2),
    Final_Titer = formatC(Final_Titer, format = "e", digits = 2),
    Survival_Rate = round(Survival_Rate, 2),
    SD = round(SD, 2)
  ) %>%
  kable(
    caption = "Summary of Virus Survival Rates by Surface and Temperature",
    col.names = c("Surface", "Phage", "Temp (°C)", 
                  "Initial Titer", "Final Titer", 
                  "Survival Rate (%)", "SD"),
    format = "html"
  ) %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"),
                full_width = FALSE) %>%
  group_rows(index = table(survival_summary$Surface))
```


```{r}
# Display results
print(survival_plot)
print(survival_table)

# Rate of decline analysis
# Calculate rate of decline for each condition
rate_analysis <- survival_data %>%
  group_by(Surface, PhageType, Temperature, Experiment) %>%
  do(model = lm(log10(SurvivalRate + 0.01) ~ Time, data = .)) %>%
  mutate(
    slope = coef(model)[2],
    r_squared = summary(model)$r.squared,
    p_value = summary(model)$coefficients[2,4]
  ) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  group_by(Surface, PhageType, Temperature) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_slope = mean(slope),
    sd_slope = sd(slope),
    mean_r2 = mean(r_squared),
    n = n()
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    half_life = -log(2)/(mean_slope * log(10)),
    decline_rate_per_hour = -(1 - 10^mean_slope) * 100
  )
```


```{r}
# Function for non-parametric analysis
# Create formatted table of decline rates
decline_table <- rate_analysis %>%
  mutate(
    mean_slope = round(mean_slope, 4),
    sd_slope = round(sd_slope, 4),
    mean_r2 = round(mean_r2, 3),
    half_life = round(half_life, 2),
    decline_rate_per_hour = round(decline_rate_per_hour, 2)
  ) %>%
  arrange(Surface, Temperature, PhageType) %>%
  kable(
    caption = "Rate of Decline Analysis by Surface, Temperature, and Phage Type",
    col.names = c("Surface", "Phage", "Temp (°C)", 
                  "Log10 Slope", "Slope SD", "R²",
                  "n", "Half-life (h)", "Decline Rate (%/h)"),
    format = "html"
  ) %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"),
                full_width = FALSE) %>%
  group_rows(index = table(rate_analysis$Surface))

# Display results
print(decline_table)
```



```{r}
# Interaction analysis
interaction_analysis <- lapply(unique(survival_data$Surface), function(s) {
  surface_data <- survival_data %>%
    filter(Surface == s, Time == 8) 
  
  # Perform two-way ANOVA
  model <- aov(SurvivalRate ~ PhageType * Temperature, data = surface_data)
  
  tidy(model) %>%
    mutate(Surface = s)
}) %>%
  bind_rows()

interaction_table <- interaction_analysis %>%
  select(Surface, term, statistic, p.value) %>%
  mutate(
    term = case_when(
      term == "PhageType" ~ "Phage Type",
      term == "Temperature" ~ "Temperature",
      term == "PhageType:Temperature" ~ "Phage × Temperature",
      TRUE ~ term
    ),
    statistic = round(statistic, 2),
    p.value = round(p.value, 4),
    significance = case_when(
      p.value < 0.001 ~ "***",
      p.value < 0.01 ~ "**",
      p.value < 0.05 ~ "*",
      p.value < 0.1 ~ ".",
      TRUE ~ "ns"
    )
  ) %>%
  filter(term != "Residuals") %>%
  arrange(Surface, term) %>%
  kable(
    caption = "Two-way ANOVA Results by Surface",
    col.names = c("Surface", "Effect", "F-statistic", "p-value", "Significance"),
    format = "html"
  ) %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"),
                full_width = FALSE) %>%
  group_rows(index = table(interaction_analysis$Surface[interaction_analysis$term != "Residuals"]))

# Display results
print(interaction_table)
```


```{r}
# 1. Data frames as CSV in Tables/KQ145
write.csv(survival_summary, "Tables/KQ145/survival_summary.csv", row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(rate_analysis, "Tables/KQ145/rate_analysis.csv", row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(interaction_analysis, "Tables/KQ145/interaction_analysis.csv", row.names = FALSE)

# 2. Lists or printed results as TXT in Tables/KQ145
assumption_text <- capture.output(print(assumption_results))
writeLines(assumption_text, "Tables/KQ145/assumption_results.txt")

statistical_text <- capture.output(print(statistical_results))
writeLines(statistical_text, "Tables/KQ145/statistical_results.txt")

decline_table_text <- capture.output(print(decline_table))
writeLines(decline_table_text, "Tables/KQ145/decline_table.txt")

interaction_table_text <- capture.output(print(interaction_table))
writeLines(interaction_table_text, "Tables/KQ145/interaction_table.txt")

survival_table_text <- capture.output(print(survival_table))
writeLines(survival_table_text, "Tables/KQ145/survival_table.txt")

```



```{r}
#Print Table in overleaf for survival rate
survival_summary <- read.csv("Tables/KQ145/survival_summary.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

n_cols <- ncol(survival_summary)
align_vec <- c("l", "l", rep("r", n_cols - 1))
digits_vec <- c(0, rep(2, n_cols))

# Create xtable
xtable_output <- xtable(
  survival_summary,
  caption = "Summary of phage survival rates across surfaces and temperatures.",
  align = align_vec,
  digits = digits_vec
)

# Print LaTeX table
print(
  xtable_output,
  include.rownames = FALSE,
  floating = TRUE,
  latex.environments = "center",
  caption.placement = "top",
  sanitize.text.function = function(x) gsub("_", "\\\\_", x, fixed = TRUE)
)
```


```{r}
survival_summary <- read.csv("Tables/KQ145/survival_summary.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
# Automatically detect columns containing "Titer"
sci_cols <- grep("titer", names(survival_summary), ignore.case = TRUE, value = TRUE)

# Apply scientific notation formatting
survival_summary[sci_cols] <- lapply(
  survival_summary[sci_cols],
  function(x) if (is.numeric(x)) formatC(x, format = "e", digits = 2) else x
)

n_cols <- ncol(survival_summary)
align_vec <- c("l", "l", rep("r", n_cols - 1))
digits_vec <- c(0, rep(2, n_cols))

# Create xtable
xtable_output <- xtable(
  survival_summary,
  caption = "Summary of phage survival rates across surfaces and temperatures.",
  align = align_vec,
  digits = digits_vec
)

# Print LaTeX table
print(
  xtable_output,
  include.rownames = FALSE,
  floating = TRUE,
  latex.environments = "center",
  caption.placement = "top",
  sanitize.text.function = function(x) gsub("_", "\\\\_", x, fixed = TRUE)
)
```


```{r}
# Plots as PNG in Figures/KQ145
ggsave("Tables/KQ145/survival_plot.png", plot = survival_plot, width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 300)

for (s_name in names(survival_plots)) {
  ggsave(paste0("Tables/KQ145/survival_plot_", s_name, ".png"), 
         plot = survival_plots[[s_name]], width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 300)
}
```


```{r}
#Create reaction norm plots for decline rates and half-lives
#Load data
df <- read.csv("rate_analysis.csv")

df <- df %>% 
  filter(!grepl("control", Surface, ignore.case = FALSE))

df <- df %>%
  mutate(Surface_Temp = paste(Surface, paste0(Temperature, "°C")))
df <- df %>%
  mutate(Surface_Temp = factor(Surface_Temp, 
    levels = df %>%
      arrange(Surface, Temperature) %>%
      distinct(Surface_Temp) %>%
      pull(Surface_Temp)
  ))

# Plot
theme_nature <- theme_bw(base_size = 14, base_family = "Helvetica") +
  theme(
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_blank(),
    axis.line = element_line(color = "black", linewidth = 0.6),
    axis.ticks = element_line(color = "black", linewidth = 0.6),
    legend.title = element_blank(),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 14),  
    legend.key.width = unit(3.3, "cm"),     
    legend.key.height = unit(0.9, "cm"),    
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 11),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 11),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5, size = 14),
    legend.position = "top",
    legend.justification = "center",
    legend.box.just = "center",
    legend.box = "horizontal"
  )
#color and line types
line_types <- c("solid", "dashed")
names(line_types) <- unique(df$PhageType)

phage_colors <- c("black", "black")
names(phage_colors) <- unique(df$PhageType)
    

# Panel a (decline rates)
p1 <- ggplot(df, aes(x = Surface_Temp, 
                     y = decline_rate_per_hour, 
                     color = PhageType, 
                     group = PhageType,
                     linetype = PhageType)) +
  geom_point(position = position_dodge(width = 0.4), size = 2.4) +
  geom_line(position = position_dodge(width = 0.4), linewidth = 1.3) +
  stat_summary(fun.data = mean_sdl, fun.args = list(mult = 1),
               geom = "errorbar", width = 0.2,
               position = position_dodge(width = 0.4)) +
  scale_linetype_manual(values = line_types, labels = c(expression(phi * "X174"), "T4")) +
  scale_color_manual(values = phage_colors, labels = c(expression(phi * "X174"), "T4")) +
  guides(
    color = guide_legend(title = NULL, nrow = 1, byrow = TRUE),
    linetype = guide_legend(title = NULL, nrow = 1, byrow = TRUE)
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Environment (Surface x Temperature)",
    y = "Decline rate (per hour)",
    title = "(a) Decline Rate Reaction Norm"
  ) +
  theme_nature

# Panel (b)Half-life
p2 <- ggplot(df, aes(x = Surface_Temp, 
                     y = half_life, 
                     color = PhageType, 
                     group = PhageType,
                     linetype = PhageType)) +
  geom_point(position = position_dodge(width = 0.4), size = 2.4) +
  geom_line(position = position_dodge(width = 0.4), linewidth = 1.3) +
  stat_summary(fun.data = mean_sdl, fun.args = list(mult = 1),
               geom = "errorbar", width = 0.2,
               position = position_dodge(width = 0.4)) +
  scale_linetype_manual(values = line_types, labels = c(expression(phi * "X174"), "T4")) +
  scale_color_manual(values = phage_colors, labels = c(expression(phi * "X174"), "T4")) +
  labs(
    x = "Environment (Surface x Temperature)",
    y = "Half-life (hours)",
    title = "(b) Half-life Reaction Norm"
  ) +
  theme_nature +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

# Combine and center legend
final_plot <- (p1 + p2) +
  plot_layout(widths = c(1, 1), guides = "collect") & 
  theme(
    legend.position = "top",
    legend.justification = "center",
    legend.box.just = "center",
    legend.box = "horizontal"
  )

# Save
ggsave("reaction_norm_rate_halflife.pdf", final_plot, width = 12, height = 5)
ggsave("reaction_norm_rate_halflife.png", final_plot, width = 12, height = 5, dpi = 600)

final_plot
```


```{r}
# Survival rate separate reaction norm figure
df <- read.csv("survival_summary.csv")
df <- df %>% 
  filter(!grepl("control", Surface, ignore.case = FALSE))
df <- df %>%
  mutate(Surface_Temp = paste(Surface, paste0(Temperature, "°C")))
df <- df %>%
  mutate(Surface_Temp = factor(Surface_Temp,
    levels = df %>%
      arrange(Surface, Temperature) %>%
      distinct(Surface_Temp) %>%
      pull(Surface_Temp)
  ))


theme_nature <- theme_bw(base_size = 14, base_family = "Helvetica") +
  theme(
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_blank(),
    axis.line = element_line(color = "black", linewidth = 0.9),
    axis.ticks = element_line(color = "black", linewidth = 0.6),
    legend.title = element_blank(),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 12),
    legend.key.width = unit(1.8, "cm"),   
    legend.key.height = unit(0.5, "cm"),  
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 11),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 11),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5, size = 14),
    legend.position = "top",
    legend.justification = "center",
    legend.box = "horizontal"
  )


line_types <- c("solid", "dashed")
names(line_types) <- unique(df$PhageType)

phage_colors <- c("PhiX174" = "black", "T4" = "black")


p <- ggplot(df, aes(x = Surface_Temp,
                    y = Survival_Rate,
                    color = PhageType,
                    group = PhageType,
                    linetype = PhageType)) +
  geom_point(position = position_dodge(width = 0.4), size = 2.2) +
  geom_line(position = position_dodge(width = 0.4), linewidth = 1.3) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = Survival_Rate - SD, ymax = Survival_Rate + SD),
                width = 0.2, position = position_dodge(width = 0.4)) +
  scale_linetype_manual(
    values = line_types,
    labels = c(expression(phi * "X174"), "T4")
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = phage_colors,
    labels = c(expression(phi * "X174"), "T4")
  ) +
  guides(
    color = guide_legend(
      title = "Phage Type",
      nrow = 1,
      byrow = TRUE,
      override.aes = list(linewidth = 1, size = 4)  
    ),
    linetype = guide_legend(
      title = "Phage Type",
      nrow = 1,
      byrow = TRUE,
      override.aes = list(linewidth = 1)
    )
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Environment (Surface × Temperature)",
    y = "Survival rate (%)",
    title = "Survival Rate Reaction Norm"
  ) +
  theme_nature

#Save
ggsave("reaction_norm_Survival_rate.pdf", p, width = 7, height = 5)
ggsave("reaction_norm_Survival_rate.png", p, width = 7, height = 5, dpi = 600)

p
```


```{r}
#Make separate reaction norm figures 
# Load data
df <- read.csv("rate_analysis.csv")

df <- df %>% 
  filter(!grepl("control", Surface, ignore.case = FALSE))

df <- df %>%
  mutate(Surface_Temp = paste(Surface, paste0(Temperature, "°C")))

df <- df %>%
  mutate(Surface_Temp = factor(Surface_Temp, 
    levels = df %>%
      arrange(Surface, Temperature) %>%
      distinct(Surface_Temp) %>%
      pull(Surface_Temp)
  ))

#ggplot style
theme_nature <- theme_bw(base_size = 14, base_family = "Helvetica") +
  theme(
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_blank(),
    axis.line = element_line(color = "black", linewidth = 1.3),
    axis.ticks = element_line(color = "black", linewidth = 0.6),
    legend.title = element_blank(),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 14),  
    legend.key.width = unit(3.3, "cm"),     
    legend.key.height = unit(0.9, "cm"),    
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 11),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 11),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5, size = 14),
    legend.position = "top",
    legend.justification = "center",
    legend.box.just = "center",
    legend.box = "horizontal"
  )

#Line types
line_types <- c("solid", "dashed")
names(line_types) <- unique(df$PhageType)

phage_colors <- c("black", "black")
names(phage_colors) <- unique(df$PhageType)

# Decline rate
declineRate <- ggplot(df, aes(x = Surface_Temp, 
                     y = decline_rate_per_hour, 
                     color = PhageType, 
                     group = PhageType,
                     linetype = PhageType)) +
  geom_point(position = position_dodge(width = 0.4), size = 2.4) +
  geom_line(position = position_dodge(width = 0.4), linewidth = 1.5) +
  stat_summary(fun.data = mean_sdl, fun.args = list(mult = 1),
               geom = "errorbar", width = 0.2,
               position = position_dodge(width = 0.4)) +
  scale_linetype_manual(values = line_types,labels = c(expression(phi * "X174"), "T4")) +
  scale_color_manual(values = phage_colors,labels = c(expression(phi * "X174"), "T4")) +
  guides(
    color = guide_legend(title = NULL, nrow = 1, byrow = TRUE),
    linetype = guide_legend(title = NULL, nrow = 1, byrow = TRUE)
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Environment (Surface × Temperature)",
    y = "Decline rate (per hour)",
    title = "Decline Rate Reaction Norm"
  ) +
  theme_nature

# === Export ===
ggsave("reaction_norm_DeclineRate.pdf", declineRate, width = 7, height = 5)
ggsave("reaction_norm_DeclineRate.png", declineRate, width = 7, height = 5, dpi = 600)

declineRate
```


```{r}
#Half-life, separate figure
df <- read.csv("rate_analysis.csv")

df <- df %>% 
  filter(!grepl("control", Surface, ignore.case = FALSE))

df <- df %>%
  mutate(Surface_Temp = paste(Surface, paste0(Temperature, "°C")))
df <- df %>%
  mutate(Surface_Temp = factor(Surface_Temp, 
    levels = df %>%
      arrange(Surface, Temperature) %>%
      distinct(Surface_Temp) %>%
      pull(Surface_Temp)
  ))


theme_nature <- theme_bw(base_size = 14, base_family = "Helvetica") +
  theme(
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_blank(),
    axis.line = element_line(color = "black", linewidth = 1.3),
    axis.ticks = element_line(color = "black", linewidth = 0.6),
    legend.title = element_blank(),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 14),
    legend.key.height = unit(0.9, "cm"),    
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 11),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 11),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5, size = 14),
    legend.position = "top",
    legend.justification = "center",
    legend.box.just = "center",
    legend.box = "horizontal"
  )


line_types <- c("solid", "dashed")
names(line_types) <- unique(df$PhageType)

phage_colors <- c("black", "black")
names(phage_colors) <- unique(df$PhageType)

#Half-life
Half_Rate <- ggplot(df, aes(x = Surface_Temp, 
                     y = half_life, 
                     color = PhageType, 
                     group = PhageType,
                     linetype = PhageType)) +
  geom_point(position = position_dodge(width = 0.4), size = 2.4) +
  geom_line(position = position_dodge(width = 0.4), linewidth = 1.5) +
  stat_summary(fun.data = mean_sdl, fun.args = list(mult = 1),
               geom = "errorbar", width = 0.2,
               position = position_dodge(width = 0.4)) +
  scale_linetype_manual(values = line_types,labels = c(expression(phi * "X174"), "T4")) +
  scale_color_manual(values = phage_colors,labels = c(expression(phi * "X174"), "T4")) +
  guides(
    color = guide_legend(title = NULL, nrow = 1, byrow = TRUE),
    linetype = guide_legend(title = NULL, nrow = 1, byrow = TRUE)
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Environment (Surface × Temperature)",
    y = "Half-life (per hour)",
    title = "Half-life Reaction Norm"
  ) +
  theme_nature

#Save
ggsave("reaction_norm_Half.pdf", Half_Rate, width = 7, height = 5)
ggsave("reaction_norm_Half.png", Half_Rate, width = 7, height = 5, dpi = 600)

Half_Rate
```


```{r}
#Differences in reproductive rates
#Effect of surface and temperature on reproduction 

# Data preparation and initial analysis
reproduction_analysis <- reproduction %>%
  group_by(PhageType, Surface, InactivationTemp, InactivationTimeHours) %>%
  arrange(ReproductionTimeMinutes) %>%
  mutate(
    # Calculate normalized titer (relative to starting point)
    NormalizedTiter = VirusTiter / first(VirusTiter),
    # Calculate growth rate between timepoints
    GrowthRate = case_when(
      ReproductionTimeMinutes > 0 ~ (VirusTiter - first(VirusTiter)) / ReproductionTimeMinutes,
      TRUE ~ 0
    ),
    # Calculate log-transformed titer for easier visualization
    LogTiter = log10(VirusTiter + 1)
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

```


```{r}
# Function to calculate standard error
calc_se <- function(x) sd(x) / sqrt(length(x))
# Enhanced growth curves with error bands
growth_plot <- reproduction_analysis %>%
  filter(InactivationTimeHours == 0) %>%  # Fresh samples only
  group_by(PhageType, Surface, InactivationTemp, ReproductionTimeMinutes) %>%
  summarise(
    MeanTiter = mean(VirusTiter),
    SE = calc_se(VirusTiter),
    .groups = 'drop'
  ) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = ReproductionTimeMinutes, 
             y = MeanTiter,
             color = PhageType,
             fill = PhageType)) +
  geom_line(size = 0.5) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = MeanTiter - SE, ymax = MeanTiter + SE), alpha = 0.2) +
  scale_y_log10(labels = scientific) +
  facet_grid(InactivationTemp ~ Surface) +
  theme_pubr() +
  labs(
    x = "Time (minutes)",
    y = "Virus Titer (PFU/mL)",
  ) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
```


```{r}
# Normalized growth curves
normalized_plot <- reproduction_analysis %>%
  filter(InactivationTimeHours == 0) %>%
  group_by(PhageType, Surface, InactivationTemp, ReproductionTimeMinutes) %>%
  summarise(
    MeanNormTiter = mean(NormalizedTiter),
    SE = calc_se(NormalizedTiter),
    .groups = 'drop'
  ) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = ReproductionTimeMinutes, 
             y = MeanNormTiter,
             color = PhageType,
             fill = PhageType)) +
  geom_line() +
  geom_point() +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = MeanNormTiter - SE, ymax = MeanNormTiter + SE), alpha = 0.2) +
  scale_y_log10(labels = comma) +
  facet_grid(InactivationTemp ~ Surface) +
  theme_pubr() +
  labs(
    x = "Time (minutes)",
    y = "Normalized Titer (Fold Change)",
  ) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

# Comprehensive metrics for comparison

final_comparison <- reproduction_analysis %>%
  filter(ReproductionTimeMinutes == 60,
         InactivationTimeHours == 0) %>%
  group_by(Surface, InactivationTemp) %>%
  summarise(
    T4_Mean = mean(VirusTiter[PhageType == "T4"]),
    Phix_Mean = mean(VirusTiter[PhageType == "Phix"]),
    T4_SE = calc_se(VirusTiter[PhageType == "T4"]),
    Phix_SE = calc_se(VirusTiter[PhageType == "Phix"]),
    TiterRatio = T4_Mean / Phix_Mean,
    LogFoldDifference = log2(TiterRatio),
    .groups = 'drop'
  )
```


```{r}
# Growth phase analysis
growth_phases <- reproduction_analysis %>%
  filter(InactivationTimeHours == 0) %>%
  group_by(PhageType, Surface, InactivationTemp) %>%
  summarise(
    EarlyGrowthRate = (VirusTiter[ReproductionTimeMinutes == 30] - VirusTiter[ReproductionTimeMinutes == 0]) / 30,
    LateGrowthRate = (VirusTiter[ReproductionTimeMinutes == 60] - VirusTiter[ReproductionTimeMinutes == 30]) / 30,
    .groups = 'drop'
  ) %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = PhageType,
    values_from = c(EarlyGrowthRate, LateGrowthRate)
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    EarlyGrowthRatio = EarlyGrowthRate_T4 / EarlyGrowthRate_Phix,
    LateGrowthRatio = LateGrowthRate_T4 / LateGrowthRate_Phix
  )

# Temperature sensitivity analysis
temp_sensitivity <- reproduction_analysis %>%
  filter(ReproductionTimeMinutes == 60,
         InactivationTimeHours == 0) %>%
  group_by(PhageType, Surface) %>%
  summarise(
    TempEffect = mean(VirusTiter[InactivationTemp == 37]) / mean(VirusTiter[InactivationTemp == 4]),
    LogTempEffect = log2(TempEffect),
    .groups = 'drop'
  )

# Combine all metrics into final table
final_table <- final_comparison %>%
  left_join(growth_phases, by = c("Surface", "InactivationTemp")) %>%
  select(Surface, InactivationTemp, TiterRatio, LogFoldDifference, EarlyGrowthRatio, LateGrowthRatio) %>%
  arrange(Surface, InactivationTemp)
```


```{r}

# Format tables
formatted_table <- final_table %>%
  kable(
    caption = "Comprehensive Comparison of T4 vs PhiX Reproduction",
    col.names = c("Surface", "Temperature (°C)", 
                  "Final Titer Ratio (T4/PhiX)",
                  "Log2 Fold Difference",
                  "Early Growth Rate Ratio",
                  "Late Growth Rate Ratio"),
    digits = 2
  ) %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"),
                full_width = FALSE)

# Temperature sensitivity table
temp_table <- temp_sensitivity %>%
  arrange(Surface) %>%
  kable(
    caption = "Temperature Sensitivity Analysis",
    col.names = c("Phage Type", "Surface", 
                  "37°C/4°C Ratio", "Log2 Fold Change"),
    digits = 2
  ) %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"),
                full_width = FALSE)

# Display results

print(growth_plot)
print(normalized_plot)
print(formatted_table)
print(temp_table)


# Summary of key findings
key_findings <- final_comparison %>%
  summarise(
    MeanTiterRatio = mean(TiterRatio),
    SDTiterRatio = sd(TiterRatio),
    MaxTiterRatio = max(TiterRatio),
    MinTiterRatio = min(TiterRatio),
    MedianLogFold = median(LogFoldDifference)
  ) %>%
  kable(caption = "Summary Statistics",
        digits = 2) %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"),
                full_width = FALSE)

print(key_findings)
```


```{r}
# Save Key Outputs
# Save data frames as CSV
write.csv(reproduction_analysis, "Tables/KQ2/reproduction_analysis.csv", row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(final_comparison, "Tables/KQ2/final_comparison.csv", row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(growth_phases, "Tables/KQ2/growth_phases.csv", row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(temp_sensitivity, "Tables/KQ2/temp_sensitivity.csv", row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(final_table, "Tables/KQ2/final_table.csv", row.names = FALSE)

# Save printed tables as TXT
formatted_table_text <- capture.output(print(formatted_table))
writeLines(formatted_table_text, "Tables/KQ2/formatted_table.txt")

temp_table_text <- capture.output(print(temp_table))
writeLines(temp_table_text, "Tables/KQ2/temp_table.txt")

key_findings_text <- capture.output(print(key_findings))
writeLines(key_findings_text, "Tables/KQ2/key_findings.txt")

# Save plots as PNG
ggsave("Tables/KQ2/growth_plot.png", plot = growth_plot, width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 300)
ggsave("Tables/KQ2/normalized_plot.png", plot = normalized_plot, width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 300)

```


```{r}
#Reproduction Reaction Norm plot
df <- read.csv("reproduction.csv")

df <- df %>%
  filter(!grepl("control", Surface, ignore.case = FALSE))


df_summary <- df %>%
  group_by(Surface, InactivationTemp, PhageType) %>%
  summarise(mean_rep = mean(VirusTiter, na.rm = TRUE),
            .groups = "drop")


df_summary <- df_summary %>%
  mutate(Surface_Temp = paste0(Surface, " ", InactivationTemp, "°C"))
df_summary <- df_summary %>%
  mutate(Surface_Temp = factor(Surface_Temp,
                               levels = df_summary %>%
                                 arrange(Surface, InactivationTemp) %>%
                                 distinct(Surface_Temp) %>%
                                 pull(Surface_Temp)))


phage_colors <- c("black", "black")
names(phage_colors) <- unique(df_summary$PhageType)
names(line_types) <- unique(df_summary$PhageType)


theme_nature <- theme_bw(base_size = 14, base_family = "Helvetica") +
  theme(
    panel.grid       = element_blank(),
    panel.border     = element_blank(),
    axis.line        = element_line(color = "black", linewidth = 0.7),
    axis.ticks       = element_line(color = "black", linewidth = 0.7),
    legend.position  = "top",
    legend.title     = element_blank(),
    legend.text      = element_text(size = 12),
    legend.key.width = unit(2.5, "cm"),     
    legend.key.height = unit(0.5, "cm"),    
    axis.text.x      = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 11),
    axis.text.y      = element_text(size = 11),
    plot.title       = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5, size = 14)
  )


# Reaction norm plot (log scale) 
p <- ggplot(df_summary,
            aes(x = Surface_Temp,
                y = mean_rep,
                color = PhageType,
                linetype = PhageType,
                group = PhageType)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.3) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  scale_y_log10() +
  scale_color_manual(values = phage_colors,
      labels = c(expression(phi * "X174"), "T4")) +
  scale_linetype_manual(values = line_types,
      labels = c(expression(phi * "X174"), "T4")) +
  guides(
    color = guide_legend(
      title = NULL,
      nrow = 1,
      byrow = TRUE,
      override.aes = list(linewidth = 1.2, size = 3) 
    ),
    linetype = guide_legend(
      title = NULL,
      nrow = 1,
      byrow = TRUE,
      override.aes = list(linewidth = 1.2)
    )
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Surface and Temperature",
    y = expression("Mean reproduction rate (log"[10]*")"),
    title = "Reproductive performance after surface exposure"
  ) +
  theme_nature

# Save
ggsave("reaction_norm_reproduction_logscale_combined.pdf", p, width = 12, height = 5)
ggsave("reaction_norm_reproduction_logscale_combined.png", p, width = 12, height = 5, dpi = 600)

p
```


```{r}
#Print Tables for overleaf
# Load data
temp_sensitivity <- read.csv("Tables/KQ2/temp_sensitivity.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

# Create and print xtable
xtable_output <- xtable(temp_sensitivity,
                       caption = "Temperature Sensitivity Analysis",
                       align = c("l", "l", "l", "r", "r"),
                       digits = c(0, 0, 0, 2, 2))

print(xtable_output,
      include.rownames = FALSE,
      floating = TRUE,
      latex.environments = "center",
      caption.placement = "top",
      sanitize.text.function = function(x) gsub("_", "\\\\_", x))

# Load data
final_table <- read.csv("Tables/KQ2/final_table.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

# Create and print xtable
xtable_output <- xtable(final_table,
                       caption = "Comprehensive Comparison of T4 vs. PhiX Reproduction",
                       align = c("l", "l", "r", "r", "r", "r", "r"),
                       digits = c(0, 0, 0, 2, 2, 2, 2))

print(xtable_output,
      include.rownames = FALSE,
      floating = TRUE,
      latex.environments = "center",
      caption.placement = "top",
      sanitize.text.function = function(x) gsub("_", "\\\\_", x))
```


```{r}
#Relationship between survival and reproduction
# Data Preparation
df <- merged_data %>%
  mutate(
    PhageType = factor(PhageType),
    Surface = factor(Surface),
    Temperature = as.numeric(Temperature),
    Time = as.numeric(Time),
    ReproductionTimeMinutes = as.numeric(ReproductionTimeMinutes),
    MeanSurfaceVirusTiter_surfaces = as.numeric(MeanSurfaceVirusTiter_surfaces),
    VirusTiter = as.numeric(VirusTiter)
  )

# Spearman Correlation
spearman_results <- df %>%
  group_by(PhageType, Temperature, Surface) %>%
  summarize(
    n = n(),
    cor_result = list(cor.test(MeanSurfaceVirusTiter_surfaces, VirusTiter, method = "spearman")),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    rho = map_dbl(cor_result, ~ .x$estimate),
    p.value = map_dbl(cor_result, ~ .x$p.value)
  ) %>%
  select(-cor_result)
# Print Spearman results
print(spearman_results)

# Load data
spearman <- read.csv("Tables/KQ3/spearman_results.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

# Format data
formatted_table <- spearman %>%
  mutate(
    p.value = signif(p.value, 3),
    rho = round(rho, 3)
  )

# Create and print xtable
xtable_output <- xtable(formatted_table,
                       caption = "Spearman Correlation Results Between Survival and Reproduction",
                       align = c("l", "l", "r", "l", "r", "r", "r"),
                       digits = c(0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 3, 3))

print(xtable_output,
      include.rownames = FALSE,
      floating = TRUE,
      latex.environments = "center",
      caption.placement = "top",
      sanitize.text.function = function(x) gsub("_", "\\\\_", x))

# Save Key Outputs
write.csv(spearman_results, "Tables/KQ3/spearman_results.csv", row.names = FALSE)
```


```{r}
#Spearman correlations reaction norm plot

df <- read.csv("spearman_results.csv")
drop_control <- TRUE
if (drop_control) {
  df <- df %>% filter(!grepl("control", Surface, ignore.case = FALSE))
}

df <- df %>%
  mutate(Temperature = factor(Temperature,
                              levels = c(4, 37),
                              labels = c("4 °C", "37 °C")))

df <- df %>%
  mutate(Surface = factor(Surface,
                          levels = unique(Surface)))  

df <- df %>%
  mutate(sig = case_when(
    p.value <= 0.001 ~ "***",
    p.value <= 0.01  ~ "**",
    p.value <= 0.05  ~ "*",
    TRUE             ~ ""
  ))
theme_nature <- theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 11),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 11),
    axis.title  = element_text(size = 12),
    plot.title  = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0, size = 14),
    strip.text  = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12),
    legend.position = "right"
  )


df$PhageType <- factor(df$PhageType, levels = c("T4", "PhiX174"))

df$PhageType <- factor(df$PhageType,
                       levels = c("T4", "PhiX174"),
                       labels = c( "T4", "plain(phi)*'X174'"))

# Heatmap
p <- ggplot(df, aes(x = Surface, y = Temperature, fill = rho)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_text(aes(label = sig), size = 4, color = "black") +
  
  scale_fill_gradient2(
    low = "#313695", mid = "white", high = "#A50026",
    midpoint = 0, limits = c(-1, 1),
    name = expression("Spearman ρ")
  ) +
  coord_fixed() +
  facet_wrap(~ PhageType, nrow = 1, labeller = label_parsed) +
  labs(
    x = "Surface",
    y = "Temperature",
    title = "Relationship between survival and reproduction"
  ) +
  theme_nature +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 13, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5, lineheight = 1.1)
  )
# Save
ggsave("spearman_heatmap_by_phage.pdf", p, width = 9, height = 4.5)
ggsave("spearman_heatmap_by_phage.png", p, width = 9, height = 4.5, dpi = 600)
p
```



```{r}
#Rate of Decline modelling (Printing for overleaf)
#Linear models to log-transformed survival rates over time to estimate slopes, half-lives, and decline rates. 

# Load the rate_analysis results from CSV
rate_analysis <- read.csv("Tables/KQ145/rate_analysis.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

# Format the data
formatted_table <- rate_analysis %>%
  mutate(
    mean_slope = round(mean_slope, 4),
    sd_slope = round(sd_slope, 4),
    mean_r2 = round(mean_r2, 3),
    half_life = round(half_life, 2),
    decline_rate_per_hour = round(decline_rate_per_hour, 2)
  ) %>%
  arrange(Surface, Temperature, PhageType)

# Create and print xtable
xtable_output <- xtable(formatted_table,
                       caption = "Rate of Decline Analysis by Surface, Temperature, and Phage Type",
                       align = c("l", "l", "l", "r", "r", "r", "r", "r", "r", "r"),
                       digits = c(0, 0, 0, 0, 4, 4, 3, 0, 2, 2))

print(xtable_output,
      include.rownames = FALSE,
      floating = TRUE,
      latex.environments = "center",
      caption.placement = "top",
      sanitize.text.function = function(x) gsub("_", "\\\\_", x))
```


```{r r interaction-table, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, results='asis'}
# Load the interaction analysis results (Printing for overleaf)
interaction_analysis <- read.csv("Tables/KQ145/interaction_analysis.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

# Format the data
formatted_table <- interaction_analysis %>%
  mutate(
    term = case_when(
      term == "PhageType" ~ "Phage Type",
      term == "Temperature" ~ "Temperature",
      term == "PhageType:Temperature" ~ "Phage × Temperature",
      TRUE ~ term
    ),
    statistic = round(statistic, 2),
    p.value = round(p.value, 4),
    significance = case_when(
      p.value < 0.001 ~ "***",
      p.value < 0.01 ~ "**",
      p.value < 0.05 ~ "*",
      p.value < 0.1 ~ ".",
      TRUE ~ "ns"
    )
  ) %>%
  filter(term != "Residuals") %>%
  select(Surface, term, statistic, p.value, significance) %>%
  arrange(Surface, term)

# Create and print xtable
xtable_output <- xtable(formatted_table,
                       caption = "Two-way ANOVA Results by Surface",
                       align = c("l", "l", "l", "r", "r", "l"),
                       digits = c(0, 0, 0, 2, 4, 0))

print(xtable_output,
      include.rownames = FALSE,
      floating = TRUE,
      latex.environments = "center",
      caption.placement = "top",
      sanitize.text.function = function(x) gsub("_", "\\\\_", x))
```
